diagrams.title: Basic ER Diagram Notation Symbols
diagrams.description: This diagram displays the fundamental symbols used in Entity-Relationship diagrams: entities (rectangles), attributes (ovals), primary key attributes (underlined), and relationships (diamonds). It helps learners recognize and distinguish between the core components of ER notation.
diagrams.purpose: Introduce the basic building blocks and visual vocabulary of ER diagram notation

diagrams.title: Cardinality and Participation Constraints
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates cardinality notations (one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many) using both Chen notation (1, N, M) and Crow's Foot notation. It also shows participation constraints (total/partial) represented by single or double lines connecting entities to relationships.
diagrams.purpose: Explain how to represent relationship cardinality and participation constraints between entities

diagrams.title: Weak Entities and Derived Attributes
diagrams.description: This diagram demonstrates advanced ER notation including weak entities (double rectangle), composite attributes (connected ovals), multivalued attributes (double oval), and derived attributes (dashed oval). It also shows identifying relationships (double diamond) that connect weak entities to their owning entities.
elements: Double Line on Weak Entity Side
diagrams.purpose: Explain advanced ER notation for weak entities, attribute types, and complex relationship modeling
